highlights: Rewrote the rover drive firmware from MicroPython to C with the Raspberry Pi Pico SDK, replacing a polling loop with a 1 kHz real-time control loop.
Added CRC-8 packet integrity, a hardware watchdog, and a Dead Man's Switch, none of which were present in the original firmware.
Designed a framed binary protocol over USB CDC and implemented the matching host-side driver as a ROS 2 node running on NVIDIA Jetson.
Built layered fail-safe behavior with communication-timeout disarming, hardware-watchdog reset, and GPIO-level motor cutoff.

projects.title: EOL Vibration Defect Detection
projects.imageAlt: Envelope spectrum with neon peaks at bearing fault frequencies
projects.subtitle: Leakage-safe ML evaluation for bearing fault diagnosis
projects.summary: A reproducible CWRU bearing-fault case study built around leakage-safe evaluation: envelope-analysis features, a physics-informed pass/fail detector, and a RandomForest whose generalization across motor load and fault severity is measured with leakage-guarded holdouts and a label-permutation sanity check.

metrics.label: fault detection rate
metrics.context: Physics-informed envelope detector with zero false alarms on healthy units (leave-one-out baseline).

metrics.label: permutation test to chance
metrics.context: Shuffled-label accuracy collapses to chance (0.28 floor), so the 0.99 in-distribution score is real signal, not leakage.

metrics.label: cross-severity accuracy
metrics.context: Mean leave-one-severity-out; 0.60 on the unseen 0.014-inch fault, the honest generalization limit.

highlights: Computed bearing fault frequencies (BPFO/BPFI/BSF/FTF) from SKF 6205 geometry and isolated fault impulses with bandpass plus Hilbert envelope analysis.
Audited the pipeline, found normal recordings leaking into both train and test, fixed the split, and added group-overlap assertions so it cannot recur.
Reported balanced accuracy and macro-F1 across rotated leave-one-load-out and leave-one-severity-out holdouts plus a label-permutation sanity check.
Tested an amplitude-invariant feature set as a cross-severity fix and reported the negative result instead of hiding it.
Made every number reproducible from one seeded evaluation script writing metrics.json, end to end inside Docker.

technicalSpecs.label: Dataset
technicalSpecs.value: CWRU Bearing Data Center, 40 recordings at 12 kHz

technicalSpecs.label: Features
technicalSpecs.value: Time-domain statistics plus Hilbert envelope band energies

technicalSpecs.label: Evaluation
technicalSpecs.value: Leakage-guarded holdouts, balanced metrics, permutation test

projects.title: Mortar Ballistic Simulation
projects.imageAlt: Parabolic mortar trajectory arc over a ranging grid
projects.subtitle: Educational exterior-ballistics demo: RK4 C engine, FastAPI, canvas
projects.summary: An educational exterior-ballistics simulator for a generic 60 mm-class projectile, built to practice numerical methods and physical modeling. A C engine integrates the point-mass trajectory with 4th-order Runge-Kutta under quadratic drag, configurable wind, and a humidity-corrected International Standard Atmosphere, exposed through a FastAPI service (the C library loaded via ctypes) with a canvas trajectory frontend. C unit tests and API tests gate the containerized build. It uses textbook physics and public parameters only, with no operational, targeting, or fire-control use.

metrics.label: trajectory integrator
metrics.context: 4th-order Runge-Kutta over the [x, y, z, vx, vy, vz] state vector, with steps down to 1 ms and ground impact interpolated to y=0.

metrics.label: simulation inputs
metrics.context: Muzzle velocity, elevation, azimuth, mass, calibre, drag coefficient, launch altitude, wind speed and direction, and humidity.

metrics.label: engine and API
metrics.context: C physics core compiled to a shared library, loaded via ctypes into a FastAPI service with a vanilla-JS canvas frontend.

highlights: Wrote the physics core in C: an RK4 integrator over the [x, y, z, vx, vy, vz] state vector with ground impact interpolated to y=0.
Modeled air density with the International Standard Atmosphere troposphere, corrected for humidity, and applied quadratic drag relative to a configurable wind.
Compiled the engine to a shared library and bridged it into a FastAPI POST /api/simulate endpoint via ctypes, with Pydantic-validated inputs.
Built a vanilla HTML/CSS/JS canvas frontend that renders the trajectory and reports range, apex, time of flight, impact velocity and angle, and lateral drift.
Containerized the stack so the C unit tests and Python API tests run during the image build, with GitHub Actions CI on every push.

technicalSpecs.label: Scope
technicalSpecs.value: Educational; generic 60 mm-class; no operational use

technicalSpecs.label: Physics core
technicalSpecs.value: C: RK4 integrator, ISA atmosphere, quadratic drag

technicalSpecs.label: State vector
technicalSpecs.value: [x, y, z, vx, vy, vz] integrated at up to 1 ms steps

technicalSpecs.label: API
technicalSpecs.value: FastAPI with a ctypes bridge to libmortar_physics.so

technicalSpecs.label: Outputs
technicalSpecs.value: Range, apex, time of flight, impact velocity/angle, drift

technicalSpecs.label: Build and CI
technicalSpecs.value: Docker multi-stage; C + Python tests gate the build; GitHub Actions

projects.title: Sparse GPU Kernels
projects.imageAlt: Sparse matrix blocks flowing through a stylized GPU compute grid
projects.subtitle: Sputnik paper reproduction across a modern GPU generation
projects.summary: Reproduced and benchmarked the Sputnik sparse-matrix CUDA kernels from Gale et al. (SC '20) on an RTX 4070 Super, validating that the paper's sparsity crossover behavior transfers across GPU generations.

metrics.label: peak SpMM throughput
metrics.context: Recorded on an NVIDIA RTX 4070 Super.

metrics.label: sparsity break-even point
metrics.context: Reproduced against the paper's approximately 71% result on V100.

highlights: Deployed the Sputnik CUDA library inside an NVIDIA NGC container.
Adapted the container configuration for current Linux kernel and NVIDIA driver versions.
Benchmarked SpMM and SDDMM kernels against cuSPARSE and dense cuBLAS baselines.
Confirmed that sparse-kernel efficiency and the break-even threshold scale across GPU generations.

technicalSpecs.label: Environment
technicalSpecs.value: NVIDIA NGC container on an updated Linux and NVIDIA driver stack

projects.title: Neural Network Inference on NVIDIA Jetson Nano
projects.imageAlt: Neural network inference graph connected to an embedded Jetson module
projects.subtitle: TensorRT model comparison and embedded GPU profiling
projects.summary: Deployed three TensorRT-optimized object-detection networks on Jetson Nano and profiled their latency and streaming-multiprocessor efficiency to identify the fastest model and the dominant post-processing bottleneck.

metrics.label: PeopleNet inference per image
metrics.context: Fastest of the three evaluated models on the embedded GPU.

metrics.label: NMS SM efficiency
metrics.context: Identified as the primary post-processing bottleneck.

highlights: Deployed PeopleNet, SSD-Inception-v2, and SSD-MobileNet-v2 with TensorRT optimization.
Compared end-to-end inference performance on the Jetson Nano's Tegra X1 GPU.
Profiled streaming-multiprocessor efficiency across each model with nvprof.
Traced the principal performance limitation to non-maximum suppression rather than convolution.

projects.title: Computer Vision Project Portfolio
projects.imageAlt: Computer vision targeting reticle analyzing a geometric scene
projects.subtitle: People counting and retinal vessel extraction
projects.summary: A two-part computer-vision portfolio covering morphology-driven crowd estimation and a Python reproduction of a hybrid retinal blood-vessel extraction pipeline evaluated on the DRIVE dataset.

metrics.label: best people-counting MAPE
metrics.context: Produced by the strongest morphological-feature method.

metrics.label: estimates within 15%
metrics.context: Share of crowd-count estimates within 15% of ground truth.

metrics.label: retinal extraction AUC
metrics.context: Measured on the DRIVE test set.

metrics.label: pixel accuracy
metrics.context: Measured on the DRIVE test set.

highlights: Extracted closing- and opening-based morphological features from foreground masks.
Trained an MLP regressor to estimate crowd count from the engineered features.
Reimplemented a MATLAB hybrid Fuzzy C-Means and supervised-classification retinal pipeline in Python.
Attributed the remaining gap from the paper's reported 0.975 AUC to its proprietary Root Guided Decision Tree, which is unavailable in standard machine-learning libraries.

technicalSpecs.label: Feature pipeline
technicalSpecs.value: Foreground masks with morphological closing and opening

technicalSpecs.label: Retinal pipeline
technicalSpecs.value: Hybrid Fuzzy C-Means plus supervised classification

technicalSpecs.label: Paper reference
technicalSpecs.value: Reported AUC 0.975 with a proprietary Root Guided Decision Tree
